rule: Must include: full name, professional email, phone number, LinkedIn URL. Optional: city/region, portfolio/GitHub. No photo, no date of birth, no marital status.
rationale: Recruiters and ATS systems parse contact info first. Missing email or phone means instant rejection.

rule: Use a professional email address (firstname.lastname@gmail.com style). Avoid nicknames, numbers, or non-business handles.
rationale: Unprofessional emails signal poor judgment to recruiters.

rule: Should have a 2-3 line professional summary that states: target role, years of experience, and 2-3 core competencies with specialization.
rationale: Recruiters spend ~7 seconds scanning a CV. Summary is the only section they always read first.

rule: Each bullet must start with a strong action verb (Led, Architected, Reduced, Increased, Delivered, Shipped). Never start with 'Responsible for', 'Worked on', 'Helped'.
rationale: Weak verbs describe duties; strong verbs describe impact. This is the #1 differentiator between junior and senior CVs.

rule: Every bullet must contain a measurable metric: numbers (%, $, count), scale (users, requests/sec), or scope (team size, budget). If no metric exists, the bullet is weak.
rationale: Metrics are what separate 'did X' from 'achieved Y'. CVs without metrics are task lists, not impact statements.

rule: Experience section should be in reverse chronological order with clear role title, company, dates (month/year), and 3-6 bullets per role.
rationale: Reverse chronological is the ATS default. Confusing order breaks parsing.

rule: Skills must be a dedicated section with concrete, named technologies/tools (Python, AWS, PostgreSQL, Kubernetes). Avoid vague terms like 'problem-solving' or 'hard worker'.
rationale: ATS systems keyword-match against job descriptions. Vague skills get filtered out. Named tools pass through.

rule: Group skills by category (Languages, Frameworks, Tools, Cloud, Databases). Avoid one massive undifferentiated list.
rationale: Grouping shows depth, not just breadth. Recruiters scan by category.

rule: Include degree, institution, graduation year, and relevant coursework or honors only for entry-level or if highly relevant. Omit high school if you have a degree.
rationale: Education section should be concise. Recency matters for coursework; irrelevance should be cut.

rule: Total length: 1 page for under 7 years experience, 2 pages otherwise. Use consistent date format, consistent bullet style, single readable font, no tables/columns/graphics that break ATS parsing.
rationale: ATS parsers fail on complex layouts. Two-page max respects recruiter time. Consistency signals professionalism.
